claim: Integration of physics-based simulations, high-throughput degradation tests and Bayesian optimization shows that perovskites centered at Cs_0.17M_A0.03FA_0.8PbI_3 exhibit minimal optical change under increased temperature, moisture and illumination achieves >100 fold stability improvement as compared to MAPbI3 while reducing the discretized sample design space by more than 1000-fold.

explanation.text: >17-fold stability improvement with >50-fold experimental reduction  was demonstrating empirically using 1.8% of the discretized (x,y) compositional space of CsxMAyFA1-x-yPbI3 (MA,methylammonium; FA, formamidinium), this claim is an exaggeration of what has been demonstrated. Refs: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.matt.2021.01.008  Matter, Volume 4, Issue 4, April 2021, pp. 1092-1094

author: str

claim: Understanding degradation of novel photovoltaics requires complex, time-consuming measurements on multiple samples. However, new machine learning models are able to accelerate degradation characterization and screening by up to 20x, accurately predicting degradation from only the first few hours of degradation.

explanation.text: The DeepDeg model significantly outperforms baseline models in forecasting the degradation of PM6 and PCE10 solar cells. It achieves a lower Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) by up to 2.46 mA/cm² when compared to benchmark models, including naive baselines and other complex models such as Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN) and LSTNet (Long- and Short-term Time-series Network). This improvement is quantified through extensive evaluation on over 13,000 hours of historical degradation data, with successful predictions validated by the model's high accuracy in capturing the JV characteristics over time. Additionally, the DeepDeg model proves its robustness through generalizability tests across different temperatures and independent holdout datasets. The model also demonstrates a remarkable speed advantage, accelerating the degradation characterization process by 5 to 20 times compared to traditional methods. Moreover, DeepDeg provides valuable insights into degradation mechanisms using KernelSHAP explanations, accurately identifying and ranking driving factors, which further validates its comprehensive capability to enhance the understanding and optimization of photovoltaic devices.

author: str
